leaderRationale: Parse failed after auto-fix attempt: ERROR: codex failed: spawn codex ENOENT
unresolved: Leader response parse failed — bug report written

conceptual_groups.summary: Parse failed: Unexpected token 'E', "ERROR: cod"... is not valid JSON
conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: LLM response was not valid JSON. Bug report written.

conceptual_groups.summary: Output paths are relative-only; self-check correctly flags that canonical directory paths are absent from <outputs>

findings.description: The <outputs> section lists artifact filenames (e.g. `<slug>-role.md`) but never specifies the canonical directory prefix `tools/agent-role-builder/role/`. Self-check raised four MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT flags for exactly this reason.

contract `package_files` values also omit directory prefix (e.g. `agent-role-builder-role.md` instead of `tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-role.md`), creating a mismatch with the filesystem layout the self-check expects.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Prepend `tools/agent-role-builder/role/` to every canonical artifact path in both the <outputs> section and the contract `package_files` block. Internal/evidence artifacts that live under a run directory can remain relative.

conceptual_groups.summary: Scope section contains a duplicate out-of-scope line that adds noise without adding clarity

findings.description: 'Application workflow creation' appears twice in the <scope> Not-in-scope list. The second occurrence adds a parenthetical but the duplication itself signals an editing error.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Collapse the two 'Application workflow creation' entries into one, keeping the clarifying parenthetical: 'Application workflow creation (role definition markdown is a role specification, not a workflow)'.

conceptual_groups.summary: Prior round 0 pushback is unresolved; the unresolved item must be addressed or explicitly documented as carried forward

findings.description: Round 0 shows Leader=pushback with 1 unresolved issue and 0 improvements. The draft does not document what that unresolved item was or show evidence it was addressed.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Either (a) document what the Round 0 pushback was and show it is resolved in this draft, or (b) if it maps to one of the findings above, cross-reference it explicitly in the decision log so the evidence chain is complete.

residual_risks: If path ambiguity in <outputs> is not fixed, a future run may write canonical artifacts to the wrong directory and the self-check will continue to fail with MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT.

The arbitration path (Step 4, 'Arbitrate after 2+ consecutive splits') is mentioned but the arbitration decision criteria and authority are not defined; a split that reaches arbitration has no documented resolution rule.
strengths: Guardrails are explicit and well-formed: freeze-only-on-full-approval, no-material-pushback-remaining, and minimum-one-Codex+Claude-pair rules are all clearly stated.
Artifact lifecycle is well-structured with canonical / evidence / internal separation and conditional vs always emission clearly marked.
Authority boundaries are precise: what is owned vs. explicitly not owned is unambiguous and consistent with the contract out_of_scope list.
Context-gathering preconditions (validate schema, verify source_refs before Step 1) are correctly sequenced.
Self-check tag enumeration in Step 3 gives an exact required tag set, making verification deterministic.
